Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Secure Domain Name System (DNS) Deployment 2026 Guide [pdf] 56 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. MSA: Memory Sparse Attention 64 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Log File Viewer for the Terminal 254 upvotes, 40 comments
  4. A 6502 disassembler with a TUI: A modern take on Regenerator 72 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. Autoresearch on an old research idea 402 upvotes, 87 comments
  6. Debunking Zswap and Zram Myths 112 upvotes, 27 comments
  7. NanoClaw Adopts OneCLI Agent Vault 81 upvotes, 19 comments
  8. BIO – The Bao I/O Co-Processor 72 upvotes, 18 comments
  9. Box of Secrets: Discreetly modding an apartment intercom to work with Apple Home 235 upvotes, 83 comments
  10. LiteLLM Python package compromised by supply-chain attack 494 upvotes, 203 comments
  11. LLM Neuroanatomy II: Modern LLM Hacking and Hints of a Universal Language? 57 upvotes, 16 comments
  12. iPhone 17 Pro Demonstrated Running a 400B LLM 682 upvotes, 307 comments
  13. Show HN: Gemini can now natively embed video, so I built sub-second video search 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  14. Ripgrep is faster than grep, ag, git grep, ucg, pt, sift (2016) 226 upvotes, 97 comments
  15. No Terms. No Conditions 31 upvotes, 8 comments
  16. curl > /dev/sda: How I made a Linux distro that runs wget | dd 106 upvotes, 42 comments
  17. LaGuardia pilots raised safety alarms months before deadly runway crash 84 upvotes, 43 comments
  18. Opera: Rewind The Web to 1996 (Opera at 30) 152 upvotes, 88 comments
  19. Testing the Swift C compatibility with Raylib (+WASM) 15 upvotes, 4 comments
  20. FCC updates covered list to include foreign-made consumer routers 418 upvotes, 283 comments
  21. Nanobrew: The fastest macOS package manager compatible with brew 74 upvotes, 41 comments
  22. Microsoft's "Fix" for Windows 11: Flowers After the Beating 685 upvotes, 510 comments
  23. io_uring, libaio performance across Linux kernels and an unexpected IOMMU trap 25 upvotes, 11 comments
  24. Hypothesis, Antithesis, Synthesis 37 upvotes, 19 comments
  25. The Jellies That Evolved a Different Way to Keep Time 14 upvotes, 5 comments
  26. No-build, no-NPM, SSR-first JavaScript framework if you hate React, love HTML 100 upvotes, 86 comments
  27. Apple Business 35 upvotes, 25 comments
  28. The bridge to wealth is being pulled up with AI 194 upvotes, 192 comments
  29. WolfGuard: WireGuard with FIPS 140-3 cryptography 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  30. So where are all the AI apps? 183 upvotes, 204 comments

Last generated 12:30am, 25 March 2026. Source here.
